merlin.size: Small falcon; wingspan 53–58 cm (21–23 in), females slightly larger than males.
merlin.habitat: Breeds in boreal forest, open moorland, and taiga; increasingly found in cities and suburbs.
merlin.diet: Primarily small birds (sparrow- to dove-sized) and medium-sized shorebirds, caught in flight.
merlin.migration: Migratory across most of its range, wintering in subtropical and northern tropical regions; some urban populations are now largely resident.

tui.size: Medium-sized passerine; one of the largest honeyeaters, roughly 27–32 cm (11–13 in) long.
tui.habitat: Forest, scrubland, and gardens throughout New Zealand, from sea level to montane zones.
tui.diet: Nectar (primary), supplemented by fruit and insects.
tui.migration: Largely resident; some seasonal altitudinal movement between lowland and upland forest.

kea.size: About 48 cm (19 in) long; a large, robust parrot with a distinctive long, curved upper beak.
kea.habitat: Forested valleys and alpine zones of the South Island of New Zealand, including subalpine scrub and rocky mountain terrain.
kea.diet: Omnivorous: roots, leaves, berries, nectar, insects, and carrion.
kea.migration: Largely resident; moves altitudinally with season and snow cover.
